# Mission Control — Claude Knowledge Base
*Auto-generated by Claude Web session. Updated: 2026-03-23*

## Project Identity
- **Name:** Mission Control
- **Owner:** Ron (levir), PST timezone. Market hours are ET (9:30am–4:00pm Mon–Fri).
- **Repo root:** `C:\Users\levir\Documents\_claude\mission-control`
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/ronle/mission-control
- **Purpose:** Multi-project management dashboard for Claude Code agents. Centralized UI to dispatch, monitor, and manage AI coding agents across all projects.

---

## Architecture

| Layer | Stack |
|-------|-------|
| Backend | Python Flask, port 5199 |
| Frontend | Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, single file: `static/index.html` (no build step) |
| Desktop | pywebview (WebView2) wrapper via `app.py`; also Tauri v2 in `src-tauri/` |
| Data | JSON files in `data/projects/`; no database |
| Agent | Spawns `claude` CLI as subprocess |

**Key files:**
- `server.py` — Flask backend, all API endpoints, agent process management
- `static/index.html` — Entire frontend SPA
- `app.py` — Desktop entry point (pywebview + Flask in daemon thread)
- `github_sync.py` — GitHub Issues bidirectional sync module
- `config.json` — User config (gitignored, auto-created)
- `data/SHARED_RULES.md` — Rules injected into all agent prompts
- `data/projects/*.json` — Per-project data (backlog, activity, settings)
- `data/schedules.json` — Scheduler config
- `docs/HIVEMIND_SPEC.md` — Hivemind feature spec (next major feature)

---

## Configuration (`config.json`)

```json
{
  "port": 5199,
  "shared_rules_path": "C:\\Users\\levir\\Documents\\_claude\\mission-control\\data\\SHARED_RULES.md",
  "projects_base": "C:\\Users\\levir",
  "user_name": "Ron",
  "use_streaming_agent": true
}
```

Other config options: `agent_model`, `agent_max_turns`, `agent_name`, `condense_threshold_kb` (30),
`condense_model` (sonnet), `condense_enabled`, `agent_channels`, `agent_remote_control`.

---

## Agent Modes

- **Mode A** (default): New `claude` process per turn. Follow-ups queue and auto-dispatch.
- **Mode B** (`use_streaming_agent: true`, currently active): Persistent process with `--input-format stream-json`. Follow-ups write to stdin. Faster. Respawn on resume if process dead.

---

## Feature Summary

### Core
- Tile-based project dashboard (snap-to-grid, drag tiles, status colors, domain tags)
- Per-project: status (Active/Waiting/Blocked/Parked), color, domain, description, path
- Multi-window system: drag, resize, minimize, tray, cascade offset, ESC closes focused

### Agent Management
- Dispatch Claude Code agents with one click; real-time SSE streaming output
- Session tabs per project (chronological), resume prior sessions via `claude -r <uuid>`
- Follow-up messages, stop/resume, send files/screenshots via paste or drag-and-drop
- AskUserQuestion: interactive radio/checkbox forms in chat
- ExitPlanMode: auto-approval button; plan file viewer (dedicated wide modal)
- Plan History tab: all historical plan .md files per project
- Terminal pop-out windows (full ANSI/xterm.js, stdin input, stop, SSE streaming)
- Token usage + cost tracking (per session + global with time range filter)
- Live elapsed timer during agent runs

### Memory System
- Native MEMORY.md at `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/memory/MEMORY.md`
- Auto-appends session summaries on completion
- MEMORY_ARCHIVE.md overflow; auto-condensation when combined > 30KB threshold
- Condensation: separate `claude -p` housekeeping process, configurable model/threshold

### Backlog
- Per-project tasks with priority (low/normal/high/critical), drag-drop reorder
- File attachments (images show preview), dispatch items to agent
- GitHub Issues bidirectional sync via `gh` CLI (auto-sync every 5 min)

### Scheduler
- Schedule types: Once (datetime), Daily (time + day-of-week), Interval (N minutes), Cron (5-field)
- Upcoming jobs banner (next 5 tasks)
- Background thread checks every 30 seconds

### Other
- Process Manager: all subprocess PIDs, live status, kill, cleanup orphaned
- Global Settings modal (5 sections; all config editable in UI)
- Per-project remote control toggle (`--remote-control` flag for claude.ai control)
- GitHub Issues sync (bidirectional, `gh` CLI, rate-limited, sanitized)
- Walkthrough tour (18 steps, auto-triggers on first run)
- Ctrl+scroll zoom on agent output, code syntax highlighting pending

## Hivemind Spec (Next Major Feature)

See `docs/HIVEMIND_SPEC.md` for full spec. Summary:

- **Hiveminds** = persistent collaborative multi-agent efforts with shared goals
- **Workstreams** = focused task areas, each owned by one agent at a time
- **Orchestrator** = Claude agent that decomposes goals, spawns workers, synthesizes findings, escalates to user
- **Workers** = standard MC agent sessions; disposable but inherit all accumulated knowledge on spawn
- **Knowledge Base** = append-only findings JSONL, decisions log, open questions, synthesis.md
- **Message Bus** = persisted inter-agent communication channel
- Key principle: *Agents are disposable; knowledge is permanent*

Data lives at `data/hiveminds/{hivemind_id}/`. Implementation phased:
1. Foundation (data + API)
2. Agent integration (orchestrator + worker system prompts, spawn lifecycle)
3. Frontend basic (tab in project modal, workstream list, escalation alerts)
4. Frontend full dashboard (standalone modal, per-workstream output, message bus viewer)
5. Intelligence & polish (auto-synthesis, cost tracking, export, templates)

---

## Agent Session Rules (MANDATORY)

**Every session startup:**
1. Get current date/time in ET. If project tag is TRADING, state market status. Otherwise skip.
2. Read last 20 lines of CHANGELOG.md. State what was last done.
3. Confirm task with Ron before starting work.

**Every session shutdown** (when Ron says "done", "stop", "close", "end", "wrap up"):
1. Stop work immediately.
2. Append to CHANGELOG.md: `## [DATE TIME] — <summary>` with Done / State / Next / Files Changed sections.
3. Update `## Current Status` in CLAUDE.md.
4. Say: *"Session documented. Safe to close."*

**Every 15 tool calls:** Re-read AGENT_RULES.md (or this file if no project rules exist).

**SHARED_RULES:** After each major update to any project artifact, update relevant docs, commit, and push to git.

**Safety:** Temp/scratch files → `temp/` only. Ask before irreversible changes. Read first, ask second, act third.
Builds: Use local Docker Desktop only. No remote CI/CD.
